content:
# Session 306 review (iter-306)

## Metadata
- Lanes: 2 prover lanes ‚Äî `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` (02KH affine brick, abstract-`B`),
  `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean` (DUAL fine-grained).
- Sorry counts: FlatBaseChange **2 ‚Üí 2** (2 new axiom-clean decls landed, the 2 deprioritized
  canonical-mate sorries untouched); DualInverse **5 ‚Üí 5** (3 sorries gained executing skeletons).
- Global sorry ~102, no net regression. Both files compile GREEN (`lean_diagnostic_messages` empty).
- `sync_leanok` ran (iter 306, sha 1c35cb8c): +4 `\leanok` in `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.

## FlatBaseChange.lean ‚Äî the 02KH brick LANDED (both bricks axiom-clean)

The iter-305 abstract-`B` recipe + one new bridge closed the brick floor (objective bars a+b).

- **`baseChangeCancelModuleIso` (L747) ‚Äî SOLVED, axiom-clean.** Abstract-`B` framing
  (`{R A R' B : CommRingCat}`, `CategoryTheory.IsPushout œÜ œà œÅ œÉ` hyp). `letI` all 5 algebra
  instances as `‚Ä¶hom.toAlgebra`; scalar towers via `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq ‚Ä¶ (fun _ => rfl)`;
  `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange ‚Ä¶ hpush M ‚Ä¶).symm`. **New ingredient**
  (vs iter-305): the categorical-pushout ‚Üí `Algebra.IsPushout` bridge
  **`CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout |>.mp h.flip`** (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Ring.Constructions`).
  The iter-305 prediction held: `keyT` becomes `rfl` with no global tensor instances to shadow.
- **`affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` (L784) ‚Äî SOLVED, axiom-clean.** The 5-step `‚â™‚â´` tilde chain
  verbatim from the recipe; type-checks first attempt (all 5 constituents already axiom-clean).
  Hypothesis `CategoryTheory.IsPushout œÜ œà œÅ œÉ` matches the `IsPullback`-of-Specs square ƒåech consumers
  carry. **Never forms the adjoint mate** (literally accurate for this decl).
- **`pushforwardSpecTildeNatIso` (M-naturality, NOT landed) ‚Äî attempted in full, removed cleanly.**
  The next concrete brick toward the gluing chain. Math worked out; two tactic walls:
  - BLOCKER 1 (rw-matching): on the `NatIso.ofComponents`-generated goal, `rw [reassoc_of% h1]`,
    `‚Üê Category.assoc`, `slice_lhs ‚Ä¶ rw [h1]` ALL fail "pattern not found" even though `h1`'s LHS is
    literally the first two factors ‚Äî the morphism objects are **defeq-but-not-syntactic**
    (`restrictScalars‚Ä¶Œì‚Ä¶` exprs). Errors: `Did not find an occurrence of the pattern (?f ‚â´ ?g) ‚â´ ?h`.
    Same class as the keyT Type-u wall, at the morphism level. NOTE: identical proof text compiled
    when `key` was a hand-written `have` whose *statement* pre-aligned the objects.
  - BLOCKER 2 (typeclass): `NatIso.isIso_of_isIso_app` whisker ‚Üí `(deterministic) timeout at typeclass
    (20000 heartbeats)` re-synthesizing `IsIso fromTildeŒì` per object. Also `isIso_fromTildeŒì_pushforward`
    is **absent from the pinned toolchain** (`Unknown identifier`), and the bare `isoWhiskerLeft`/
    `isoWhiskerRight` were renamed to the `Functor.`-method form (`Unknown identifier`).
  - **Fix for iter-307** (from task_result): build the NatIso **purely compositionally**
    (`Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (tilde.functor R') (gammaPushforwardNatIso œÜ) ‚â™‚â´ associator.symm ‚â™‚â´
    Functor.isoWhiskerRight tilde.toTildeŒìNatIso.symm (restrictScalars œÜ.hom) ‚â™‚â´ leftUnitor _`) so no
    naturality square is ever stated ‚Üí sidesteps BLOCKER 1; register the `fromTildeŒì` IsIso via the
    essImage witness `isIso_fromTildeŒì_iff.mpr ‚ü®restrictScalars œÜ M, ‚ü®(pushforward_spec_tilde_iso œÜ M).symm‚ü©‚ü©`
    as a named hyp fed by lambda ‚Üí sidesteps BLOCKER 2.

## DualInverse.lean ‚Äî 3/5 sorries gained skeletons; the "wall" narrative is FALSE

- **`left_inv` / `right_inv` (refine_5/6) ‚Äî PARTIAL, real advance.** `apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext;
  intro W''` drops each round-trip to a per-component equality, **provably bypassing** the naturality
  fields (proof-irrelevant under `hom_ext`). Residual is a 4-leg telescope closeable by the existing
  Œµ-cancellation lemmas (`dualUnitRingSwap_comp_dualUnitRingSwapInv`, `‚Ä¶Inv_comp_‚Ä¶`, `Iso.inv_hom_id`)
  + `eqToHom`/`restrictScalarsId'App` collapse. bare sorry ‚Üí executing skeleton. **No new infra needed.**
- **`naturality` (refine_1, L533), `sliceDualTransportInv` naturality (L388), `dual_restrict_iso`
  naturality (L760) ‚Äî prover declared an "ARCHITECTURAL WALL".** Claim (Lean comment L525-532 +
  a new project memory note): ingredient (b), the Œµ-naturality of `restrictScalars`, needs
  `PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxŒµ` which "does NOT exist in the codebase" and requires
  "substantial monoidal infrastructure".

  **THIS CLAIM IS FALSE (review-falsified, see below).** `restrictScalarsLaxŒµ` exists, is
  axiom-clean, and is directly imported by DualInverse.lean. This is the headline finding of the
  session.

## ‚öÝ HEADLINE FINDING ‚Äî the DualInverse "architectural wall" is a false premise

The prover ran `lean_local_search restrictScalarsLax` ‚Üí empty and concluded the Œµ-NatTrans is absent.
It is not:
- **`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxŒµ` is defined at `PresheafInternalHom.lean:290`** (with
  `restrictScalarsLaxŒº` L306 and the `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` instance L323), is **axiom-clean**,
  and **carries a proved `NatTrans.naturality` field**.
- **DualInverse.lean directly imports it** ‚Äî `import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate.PresheafInternalHom`
  on **line 7**. So it is in scope at the open sorries.
- The blueprint references it correctly as `def:restrict_scalars_lax_epsilon` (`\leanok`).
- `lean-vs-blueprint-checker` (dual306, fresh opus read of both files): "its naturality field is
  exactly the across-two-ring-maps square the comment says is missing‚Ä¶ flagged as actively
  misleading‚Ä¶ could send the next prover to rebuild existing infra."

Consequences to fix (planner; review cannot edit `.lean`/project-memory):
1. The Lean comment `DualInverse.lean:525-532` ("does NOT exist in the codebase", "ARCHITECTURAL
   WALL", "substantial monoidal infrastructure") is wrong ‚Üí correct it.
2. The new project memory note `memory/dualinverse-naturality-wall.md` (+ its `MEMORY.md` index line)
   added THIS iter asserts the same false claim ‚Üí delete/correct it before it poisons future iters.
3. iter-307: re-open the 3 naturality sorries with `restrictScalarsLaxŒµ` in hand ‚Äî **not** as a wall.

## Subagent reports
- `lean-auditor` (iter306): 0 critical, 4 major, 6 minor. Both FlatBaseChange decls clean; all 5
  DualInverse sorries honest. Majors: stale planning comments (`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`
  inline status 64 iters stale ‚Üí iter-242; module docstring 72 iters stale ‚Üí iter-234/236);
  DualInverse `sliceDualTransport` ~3:1 comment-to-code (~155 comment / ~50 code). Note the auditor
  (no blueprint context) accepted the "architectural wall" as a real infra gap ‚Äî the
  lean-vs-blueprint-checker, which read PresheafInternalHom, is the one that caught it false.
  Report: `task_results/lean-auditor-iter306.md`.
- `lean-vs-blueprint-checker` (fbc306): both landed decls **faithful + axiom-clean**, 0 red flags,
  1 minor (add `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout` anchor to the `lem:baseChangeCancelModuleIso`
  proof prose). `sec:fbc_gluing` confirmed not-started. Report: `task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-fbc306.md`.
- `lean-vs-blueprint-checker` (dual306): the headline falsification above + secondary: `sliceDualTransportInv`
  carries an `hŒ≤` hyp the blueprint omits; the blueprint's reverse-component formula is thinner than
  the closed Lean `app` (omits cross-fiber `restrictScalars œÅ`/`collapse`/`unitRelabelSwap`);
  `unitRelabelSwap` and `isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_presheafMap` lack `\lean{}` blocks. Forward lane
  excellently guided. Report: `task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-dual306.md`.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`, `lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib`: confirmed `\mathlibok` (anchor
  `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`) ‚Äî left as-is (already correct from iter-305).
- `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`, `lem:baseChangeCancelModuleIso`: added `% NOTE:` recording the
  `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout` bridge as the load-bearing anchor (checker minor; the proof
  prose otherwise names the transport only verbally).
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, `def:restrict_scalars_lax_epsilon`: added `% NOTE:` flagging that
  the Lean side (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxŒµ`, PresheafInternalHom.lean:290) EXISTS and
  is imported by DualInverse ‚Äî countering the prover's false "absent" claim for the next iter.
- No `\leanok` overrides (sync_leanok's +4 on the two FlatBaseChange decls is correct).

## Doctor / coverage
- blueprint-doctor: broken `\cref`/`\uses` ‚Äî `Picard_GlueDescent.tex` (2), `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`
  (13). Pre-existing, off the active front, unchanged from iter-305. Gate opening `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`
  for the planned RR-free Quot lane.
- Coverage debt: `dag-query unmatched` = 371, unchanged. The 2 new FlatBaseChange decls already carry
  `\lean{}`-pinned blueprint blocks (`lem:baseChangeCancelModuleIso` L931, `lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`
  L964); they appear in the unmatched snapshot only because the DAG is rebuilt at the next plan phase.
  No new `lean_aux` helper landed this iter (the probe was removed).
